title: Sequences, expressions and formulae

content.raw_text: a The first term of a sequence is 4. The term-to-term rule of the sequence is 'subtract 3'. Write down the first three terms of the sequence. b The position-to-term rule of a sequence is: term = 4 x position number + 1. Work out the first three terms of the sequence.
content.latex: extbf{a} 	ext{ The first term of a sequence is 4. The term-to-term rule of the sequence is 'subtract 3'. Write down the first three terms of the sequence.} \ 	extbf{b} 	ext{ The position-to-term rule of a sequence is: term } = 4 	imes 	ext{ position number } + 1. 	ext{ Work out the first three terms of the sequence.}

source_refs.note: Example showing term-to-term and position-to-term rules.

text.raw_text: Write down the first three terms of each sequence.
text.latex: ext{Write down the first three terms of each sequence.}

text.latex: ext{first term: 1, term-to-term rule: 'add 5'}

text.latex: ext{first term: 20, term-to-term rule: 'subtract 4'}

text.latex: ext{first term: 2, term-to-term rule: 'add 12'}

text.latex: ext{first term: 6, term-to-term rule: 'subtract 5'}

text.latex: ext{first term: -3, term-to-term rule: 'subtract 6'}

text.raw_text: The first term of a sequence is 15. The term-to-term rule is add 7. What is the fifth term of the sequence? Explain how you worked out your answer.
text.latex: ext{The first term of a sequence is 15. The term-to-term rule is add 7. What is the fifth term of the sequence? Explain how you worked out your answer.}

text.raw_text: This is part of Zalika’s homework. a Is Zalika’s answer correct? b Explain how her method works. c The first term of a sequence is 5. The term-to-term rule is ‘add 8’. Use Zalika’s method to work out: i the 10th term ii the 20th term iii the 50th term of the sequence.
text.latex: ext{This is part of Zalika’s homework.} \ 	extbf{a} 	ext{ Is Zalika’s answer correct?} \ 	extbf{b} 	ext{ Explain how her method works.} \ 	extbf{c} 	ext{ The first term of a sequence is 5. The term-to-term rule is ‘add 8’. Use Zalika’s method to work out: } \ 	extbf{i} 	ext{ the 10th term } 	extbf{ii} 	ext{ the 20th term } 	extbf{iii} 	ext{ the 50th term of the sequence.}

text.raw_text: Ahmad is trying to solve this problem: 'The fourth term of a sequence is 39. The term-to-term rule is ‘add 7’. Work out the first term of the sequence.' Work out the answer to the problem. Explain how you solved the problem.
text.latex: ext{Ahmad is trying to solve this problem: 'The fourth term of a sequence is 39. The term-to-term rule is ‘add 7’. Work out the first term of the sequence.' Work out the answer to the problem. Explain how you solved the problem.}

text.raw_text: Mia is trying to solve this problem: 'The 12th term of a sequence is 16. The term-to-term rule is ‘subtract 3’. Work out the third term of the sequence.' Work out the answer to the problem. Explain how you solved the problem.
text.latex: ext{Mia is trying to solve this problem: 'The 12th term of a sequence is 16. The term-to-term rule is ‘subtract 3’. Work out the third term of the sequence.' Work out the answer to the problem. Explain how you solved the problem.}

text.raw_text: The table shows two of the terms in a sequence. Position-to-term rule: term = position number + 7. Copy the table. Use the position-to-term rule to work out the missing numbers. (Table: Pos 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 10, 20 | Term: null, 9, null, null, null, 17, null)
text.latex: ext{The table shows two of the terms in a sequence. Position-to-term rule: term } = 	ext{ position number } + 7. 	ext{ Copy the table. Use the position-to-term rule to work out the missing numbers.}

text.raw_text: Use the position-to-term rule to work out the first four terms of each sequence.
text.latex: ext{Use the position-to-term rule to work out the first four terms of each sequence.}

text.raw_text: term = 6 x position number
text.latex: ext{term } = 6 	imes 	ext{ position number}

text.raw_text: term = position number - 4
text.latex: ext{term } = 	ext{ position number } - 4

text.raw_text: term = 2 x position number + 1
text.latex: ext{term } = 2 	imes 	ext{ position number } + 1

text.raw_text: term = 3 x position number - 1
text.latex: ext{term } = 3 	imes 	ext{ position number } - 1

text.raw_text: Use the position-to-term rules to work out: i the 10th term, ii the 20th term, iii the 100th term of each sequence.
text.latex: ext{Use the position-to-term rules to work out: i the 10th term, ii the 20th term, iii the 100th term of each sequence.}

text.raw_text: term = position number + 5
text.latex: ext{term } = 	ext{ position number } + 5

text.raw_text: term = 2 x position number
text.latex: ext{term } = 2 	imes 	ext{ position number}

text.raw_text: term = 4 x position number + 8
text.latex: ext{term } = 4 	imes 	ext{ position number } + 8

text.raw_text: term = 5 x position number - 25
text.latex: ext{term } = 5 	imes 	ext{ position number } - 25

text.raw_text: The fourth term of a sequence is 11. The seventh term of the sequence is 20. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence? A term = position number + 7; B term = 2 x position number + 3; C term = 3 x position number - 1; D term = 4 x position number - 5. Show how you worked out your answer.
text.latex: ext{The fourth term of a sequence is 11. The seventh term of the sequence is 20. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence? } \ 	extbf{A} 	ext{ term } = 	ext{ position number } + 7; \ 	extbf{B} 	ext{ term } = 2 	imes 	ext{ position number } + 3; \ 	extbf{C} 	ext{ term } = 3 	imes 	ext{ position number } - 1; \ 	extbf{D} 	ext{ term } = 4 	imes 	ext{ position number } - 5. \ 	ext{Show how you worked out your answer.}

text.raw_text: This is part of Razi’s homework. Question: 'The position-to-term rule of a sequence is: term = 3 x position number + 2. Work out the first three terms of the sequence.' Razi's Answer: 1st term = 5, 2nd term = 17, 3rd term = 53. Is Razi’s homework correct? Explain your answer.
text.latex: ext{This is part of Razi’s homework. Question: 'The position-to-term rule of a sequence is: term } = 3 	imes 	ext{ position number } + 2. 	ext{ Work out the first three terms of the sequence.' Razi's Answer: 1st term } = 5, 	ext{ 2nd term } = 17, 	ext{ 3rd term } = 53. 	ext{ Is Razi’s homework correct? Explain your answer.}

text.raw_text: Write down the first three terms of each of the sequences described.
text.latex: ext{Write down the first three terms of each of the sequences described.}

text.raw_text: first term is 7, term-to-term rule is ‘add 3’
text.latex: ext{first term is 7, term-to-term rule is ‘add 3’}

text.raw_text: first term is 11, term-to-term rule is ‘subtract 5’
text.latex: ext{first term is 11, term-to-term rule is ‘subtract 5’}

text.raw_text: position-to-term rule is term = 8 x position number
text.latex: ext{position-to-term rule is term } = 8 	imes 	ext{ position number}

text.raw_text: position-to-term rule is term = 4 x position number - 3
text.latex: ext{position-to-term rule is term } = 4 	imes 	ext{ position number } - 3

text.raw_text: The third term of a sequence is 9. The eighth term of the sequence is 19. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence? A term = position number + 9; B term = 2 x position number + 3; C term = 3 x position number; D term = 4 x position number - 3. Show how you worked out your answer.
text.latex: ext{The third term of a sequence is 9. The eighth term of the sequence is 19. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence? } \ 	extbf{A} 	ext{ term } = 	ext{ position number } + 9; \ 	extbf{B} 	ext{ term } = 2 	imes 	ext{ position number } + 3; \ 	extbf{C} 	ext{ term } = 3 	imes 	ext{ position number}; \ 	extbf{D} 	ext{ term } = 4 	imes 	ext{ position number } - 3. \ 	ext{Show how you worked out your answer.}

text.raw_text: For each sequence of numbers: i write down the term-to-term rule, ii write the sequence of numbers in a table, iii work out the position-to-term rule, iv check your rule works for the first three terms.
text.latex: ext{For each sequence of numbers: i write down the term-to-term rule, ii write the sequence of numbers in a table, iii work out the position-to-term rule, iv check your rule works for the first three terms.}

text.raw_text: This pattern is made from dots. (Image of 4 patterns). Alicia thinks that the nth term for the sequence of numbers of dots is 3n + 3. Is Alicia correct? Explain how you worked out your answer.
text.latex: ext{This pattern is made from dots. Alicia thinks that the } n	ext{th term for the sequence of numbers of dots is } 3n + 3. 	ext{ Is Alicia correct? Explain how you worked out your answer.}

text.raw_text: a Copy and complete the table of values for each function machine. i x -> +9 -> y. ii x -> *2 -> -3 -> y. b Write each of the functions in part a as an equation.
text.latex: extbf{a} 	ext{ Copy and complete the table of values for each function machine. i } x 	o +9 	o y. 	ext{ ii } x 	o 	imes 2 	o -3 	o y. \ 	extbf{b} 	ext{ Write each of the functions in part a as an equation.}

text.latex: ext{multiplies the number by 2 then adds 7}

text.latex: x + 7 	ext{ when } x = -12

text.latex: 3x - 4y 	ext{ when } x = 2 	ext{ and } y = 7

text.latex: 3n + 1 	ext{ when } n = -6

text.latex: 30 - 5k 	ext{ when } k = -2

text.latex: x^2 - 10 	ext{ when } x = 3

text.latex: 4y^3 	ext{ when } y = 2

text.raw_text: Use the formula P = mgh to work out P when m = 5, g = 10 and h = 3.
text.latex: ext{Use the formula } P = mgh 	ext{ to work out } P 	ext{ when } m = 5, g = 10 	ext{ and } h = 3.

text.raw_text: Zalika uses the formula v^2 = u^2 + 2as. When she substitutes v = 4, u = 3 and a = 7 into the formula she gets the equation 8 = 6 + 14s. Is Zalika correct? Show how you worked out your answer.
text.latex: ext{Zalika uses the formula } v^2 = u^2 + 2as. 	ext{ When she substitutes } v = 4, u = 3 	ext{ and } a = 7 	ext{ into the formula she gets the equation } 8 = 6 + 14s. 	ext{ Is Zalika correct? Show how you worked out your answer.}

notes: Question 10 on page 20 was captured as a single conceptual question about Razi's homework accuracy.
Questions with complex visual aids like dot patterns and function machines (Q4, Q5 on p.30) are described textually.
Mathematical expressions were formatted using LaTeX for consistency and readability.
